availabilityVersion.description: Client-visible freshness token for the exact request context. Exact slots are reusable only when this matches.

generatedAt.description: ISO timestamp for when the payload was generated.

professionalId.description: Transitional duplicate fields. Prefer `request.*` in all new code.

selectedDay.description: Canonical enterprise field. Authoritative for first paint because it belongs to the current bootstrap response.

availabilityVersion.description: Client-visible freshness token for the exact request context. Exact slots are reusable only when this matches.

generatedAt.description: ISO timestamp for when the payload was generated.

professionalId.description: Transitional duplicate fields. Prefer `request.*` in all new code.

availabilityVersion.description: Client-visible freshness token for the exact request context. Exact slots are reusable only when this matches.

generatedAt.description: ISO timestamp for when the payload was generated.

AvailabilityLocationOption.description: One bookable salon/suite option for the primary pro. Present so the client can choose which location to visit when the pro has several. Empty for mobile mode.

offeringId.description: Optional on the generic UI card because primary vs alternate cards have slightly different guarantees. Use the specialized types below when availability context must guarantee bookability.

slots.description: UI convenience only. Do not treat these as globally authoritative without request/version validation.

offeringId.description: Optional on the generic UI card because primary vs alternate cards have slightly different guarantees. Use the specialized types below when availability context must guarantee bookability.

slots.description: UI convenience only. Do not treat these as globally authoritative without request/version validation.

BookingBeforeAfterThumbs.description:
The primary "before" and "after" photo for a booking, already resolved to renderable URLs (signed for the private session bucket, public otherwise).

This is the single source of truth for the before/after pair shown anywhere that links to a client's aftercare summary (home action card, aftercare inbox, …) — so those surfaces don't each re-implement the media select + phase pick + URL rendering.

ChannelPreferenceState.description:
Read/write surface for the *NotificationPreference tables. The delivery engine already consumes these rows (see channelPolicy.ts); this module only lets a user view and edit their own rows. It changes no policy default.

Quiet hours are stored per-eventKey on every preference row, so a single user-facing window is written uniformly across all of an owner's rows.

To tell "never configured" apart from "explicitly turned off", we use a sentinel the engine already treats as no-quiet-hours: equal start/end minutes (channelPolicy.isWithinQuietHours returns false when start === end). So:   - both null               → never configured → UI default ON (22:00–08:00)   - start === end (e.g. 0)  → explicitly OFF   - start !== end           → explicitly ON with that window

depositAmount.description: Decimal deposit amount string (e.g. "25.00"), formatted client-side like the other checkout amounts. Null when no deposit applies.

depositStatus.description: Discovery deposit lifecycle (BookingDepositStatus): NONE · PENDING · PAID · REFUNDED · FAILED. A deposit is OWED-and-unpaid exactly when this is "PENDING" — that's the gate for a "Pay deposit" CTA. Populated only where the source query selects the deposit columns (the client bookings list route); null elsewhere.

hasPendingRebookConfirmation.description: True when the pro proposed a next appointment (aftercare BOOKED_NEXT_APPOINTMENT) the client hasn't confirmed or declined yet — drives the rebook-confirm CTA. Confirm/decline via POST /api/v1/client/bookings/[id]/aftercare-rebook { action }. Populated only where the source query selects the aftercare/rebook columns (the client bookings list route); false elsewhere.

mediaUseConsent.description: True when the client has granted the pro media-use consent for this session (allow featuring their photos/video publicly — portfolio/Looks). Toggle via POST /api/v1/client/bookings/[id]/media-consent { granted }. Populated only where the source query selects `mediaUseConsentAt` (the client bookings list route); false elsewhere.

rebookProposedFor.description: The pro-proposed next-appointment instant (ISO) when one is pending; else null.

lookName.description: The author's look that inspired the booking.

proName.description: The pro the remix was booked with.

who.description: Booker, PII-safe: `@handle` for a public client, else a generic label.

creator.description: Real-data creator metrics + remixes. `isCreator` gates the whole creator UI: false until the client has published at least one authored look.

data.description: Structured, event-specific metadata. Non-object payloads serialize to null.

readAt.description: ISO timestamp when the client read it, or null while unread.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null when the feed is exhausted.

JsonObject.description: From https://github.com/sindresorhus/type-fest/ Matches a JSON object. This type can be useful to enforce some input to be JSON-compatible or as a super-type to be extended from.

JsonValue.description: From https://github.com/sindresorhus/type-fest/ Matches any valid JSON value.

emailLocked.description: When true, the EMAIL toggle is locked on — this is a critical event (e.g. a payment receipt) whose email can never be turned off. The engine enforces this regardless of the saved preference; the UI just reflects it.

supportedChannels.description: Channels this event can use for this audience (the only toggles shown).

group.description: Add-on grouping label (e.g. "Color"), or null when ungrouped.

id.description: OfferingAddOn link id — the value to send back in finalize's `addOnIds`.

serviceId.description: The underlying add-on service id (not what finalize wants — use `id`).

defaultAddressId.description: Default client mobile address (only resolved for an authed mobile claim).

services.description: JSON-safe (Decimal -> string) services + incentive blocks for native clients.

following.description: The signed-in client already follows this profile.

isOwn.description: The signed-in client is looking at their OWN profile.

lastSeenAt.description: Most recent push-token activity for this device (ISO), or null.

pushActive.description: At least one push token for this device is still active.

revoked.description: This device's session has been revoked.

revokedAt.description: When the device session was revoked (ISO), or null.
